message:
fix(tests): convert standalone binaries to proper #[test] fns and complete assertions

Five test files cleaned up:

stream.rs: Prefix two unused match bindings with underscore
(other → _other) to suppress dead-code warnings in
poll_frame_after_trailers and poll_frame_multipart_trailers tests.

notify_bug_test.rs: Convert from standalone main() binary to a
proper #[test] function registered in sync/mod.rs as a #[cfg(test)]
module. Replace external crate import (asupersync::sync::Notify)
with crate-relative (crate::sync::Notify). Replace println-based
output with assert!() and assert_eq!() calls that verify the
baton-pass behavior: notify_one wakes waiter 1, dropping waiter 1
passes baton to waiter 2, and waiter 2 resolves to Ready.

lost_wakeup_bug.rs: Convert from main() to #[test] fn
mutex_waiter_chain_does_not_lose_wakeup. Replace unsafe
Pin::new_unchecked with safe Pin::new (future is Unpin).
Replace all println debug output with assert_eq! calls verifying
waiter counts at each step and a final assert that W3 acquires
the lock after W1 and W2 are dropped. Add doc comment explaining
the regression target.

rate_limit_leak.rs: Complete the previously skeletal test with
actual assertions. The cancel_entry API now takes a timestamp
parameter. After cancelling entry 1, call process_queue at a
later time and assert it grants entry 2 (not the cancelled
entry 1), proving cancelled entries don't leak in the wait queue.

sync/mod.rs: Add #[cfg(test)] mod notify_bug_test so the
converted test is discovered by cargo test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
